Description: Beachfront retreat with 30 private suites surrounded by lush nature. Aman Resorts' Sri Lankan property combines understated luxury with pristine coastline. Each suite features a private plunge pool and direct beach access.
Tags: private plunge pools in every suite

Description: Dilmah's flagship resort perched on a 60-foot cliff with panoramic Indian Ocean views. 39 individually designed villas, crescent-shaped infinity pool (one of Sri Lanka's largest), and world-class dining by Dharshan Munidasa.

Luxury tented camp at Yala National Park's border. 36 cocoon-shaped tents inspired by local geology — designed by renowned architect Murad Ismail. Canvas-walled suites with plunge pools, copper bathtubs, and panoramic wilderness views. Leopard sightings from your deck.

Only 14 luxury cabins at the edge of Yala National Park. Each hut is a stand-alone structure with thatched roof, outdoor shower, and private plunge pool — designed to blend into the jungle. Game drives, forest bathing, and starlit dining.

1929 heritage mansion in Cinnamon Gardens — former residence of the Bandaranaike family (three Prime Ministers lived here). 10 individually designed suites, tropical courtyard with lap pool, and the legendary Gallery Café. Hosted King Charles III in 2013.
USP: Royal pedigree — hosted King Charles III

Description: Pioneering eco-boutique hotel built on a reclaimed paddy field transformed into an artificial wetland. Over-water bungalows, reed-thatched roofs, paddy field views, and Sigiriya Rock on the horizon. Conde Nast Top 15 Eco-Hotels Worldwide.

Award-winning wellness retreat in the Knuckles Mountain Range. 25 minimalist villas carved into the hillside overlooking misty valleys. Full Ayurvedic programs, yoga pavilion, thermal springs, farm-to-table cuisine. Named 'World's Best Wellness Retreat' by multiple publications.

17th-century Dutch merchant's mansion transformed into a 12-room boutique hotel within the UNESCO Galle Fort. Antique four-poster beds, courtyard dining, cinnamon-themed spa, and curated heritage walks through the Fort.

Perched on a tea plantation in Ella with uninterrupted views of Ella Gap and Little Adam's Peak. 29 rooms including glass-walled suites and treehouse-style villas. Infinity pool overlooking the valley, Sri Lankan cuisine, and walking distance to Ella's main attractions.

Sri Lanka's first boutique hotel (est. 1995). 27 suites in two-storey villas with private pools, perched on a rocky promontory between Bentota beach and the Indian Ocean. Topless sunbathing allowed, Ayurvedic spa, and curated art collection.

Teardrop Hotels' Galle Fort property — a 14-room luxury boutique housed in a restored 18th-century Dutch home. Minimalist interiors with antique accents, central courtyard with plunge pool, and the acclaimed Church Street Social restaurant. Walking distance to Galle Lighthouse.

Aman Resorts' Galle property set in a 1684 Dutch colonial building within the UNESCO Fort. 30 rooms and suites with high ceilings, antique furnishings, and a grand verandah overlooking the Fort ramparts. The oldest hotel in Galle (operating since 1865 as the New Oriental Hotel).

18-room luxury boutique on 7 acres of tropical gardens near Colombo Airport. Mountbatten Suite with private plunge pool, daily guided meditation, afternoon tea included. Often called 'the best airport hotel in the world'. The perfect first/last night in Sri Lanka.
USP: Best airport hotel in the world

Description: 1804 heritage manor house surrounded by terraced paddy fields and tropical gardens. 9 suites in the main house plus garden cottages. Four-poster beds, antique verandahs, infinity pool overlooking rice paddies. The ultimate Kandy hideaway.

10 individual villas on the shores of Koggala Lake near Galle. Modern minimalist design with floor-to-ceiling glass, private plunge pools, outdoor showers, and a spectacular lakeside infinity deck. Sustainability-focused with solar power and organic gardens.

Conde Nast recommended — just 5 rooms in a beautifully restored colonial villa in Weligama. Heritage charm with lush gardens, a stunning pool, and authentic Sri Lankan hospitality. Walking distance to Weligama Beach and surf breaks.

An utterly unique Art Nouveau boutique hotel in Kandy — think Alice in Wonderland meets Salvador Dali. Every surface is covered in murals, paintings, and eccentric decor by artist Helga de Silva Blow Perera. 14 themed rooms, each completely different. A guestbook signed by Gregory Peck, Laurence Olivier, and Vivien Leigh.

Uga Escapes' urban flagship — a small, design-forward luxury property in Colombo city. Part of the acclaimed Uga Escapes collection (also includes Chena Huts, Ulagalla). Personalized service in a private residence setting.

An 8-room heritage boutique hotel on the same street as Tintagel in Colombo 7. Restored colonial mansion with high ceilings, antiques, and a serene courtyard. Intimate and quiet — a hidden gem in the city's diplomatic quarter.

Geoffrey Bawa's last hotel project — a 5-star boutique resort on Wadduwa's golden coastline. 69 rooms laid out in a sweeping curve along the beach. Bawa's signature indoor-outdoor design, lagoon-style pools, and tropical landscaped gardens.

A stunning 8-room boutique beachfront villa in Tangalle. White-washed walls, four-poster beds, private pool overlooking the beach, and a relaxed barefoot-luxury vibe. The closest boutique alternative to Anantara in Tangalle.
